title: Filtering, Three-Valued Logic, Patterns, Ordering, and Pagination · Query Retrieval
summary: A queue view must include the correct unresolved tickets, handle missing owners, and remain deterministic across pages. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a deterministic queue-filter query suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.
Predict TRUE, FALSE, and UNKNOWN through comparisons and boolean operators involving NULL.
Use IS NULL, IS NOT NULL, COALESCE, and NULLIF only when their business meaning is justified.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Order Limit Pagination
steps.instruction:
review case — Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 5, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. Competency probe: Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs.

Inspect the Order Limit Pagination trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
